title: Standard cell potential (E°cell)
short: The voltage a galvanic cell produces under standard conditions, found from the two half-cell potentials.
full:
A galvanic (voltaic) cell turns a spontaneous redox reaction into electrical work. Its standard cell potential is the difference between the standard reduction potentials of the cathode (reduction) and the anode (oxidation): E°cell = E°cathode − E°anode.

A positive E°cell means the reaction as written is spontaneous. Standard reduction potentials are tabulated against the standard hydrogen electrode, so any two half-reactions can be combined; the half-reaction with the higher reduction potential becomes the cathode.
